rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless): 14/14 auto-checkable pass (loads-clean, calls-real-inference, …). Real full-lifecycle run in headless Chromium with a persistent profile: first visit showed state=download-required with a Download button; after downloading ~250 MB the pipeline('text2text-generation','Xenova/t5-base',{device:'wasm',dtype:'q8'}) produced REAL output for the default translate task — 'translate English to German: The house is wonderful and the garden is quiet.' → 'Das Haus ist wunderbar und der Garten ist ruhig.' 0 console errors. All four prefixes verified separately in-browser (WASM q8): translate DE 'Das Haus ist wunderbar.', summarize → coherent summary, 'cola sentence: The books is on the table.' → 'unacceptable', stsb → '3.2'.
rubric.notes: This is the ORIGINAL task-prefix T5, distinct from the instruction-tuned flan-t5-instruct demo. The prefix is the task selector; the same weights do four jobs.

rubric.evidence: conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual. Verified per task: translate emits target-language text preserving meaning (DE/FR/RO), summarize emits a shorter faithful text, CoLA emits the single word acceptable/unacceptable, STS-B emits a 0–5 number. Each output is labelled with its expected shape via task.outputHint. Real samples above.
rubric.notes: At q8 the base model is genuinely capable at translate/summarize; CoLA/STS-B verdicts are shown honestly (STS-B is a float string).

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (Xenova/t5-base, linked to card), declares-task (text2text-generation), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker: t5.js T5Engine → worker.js) all pass. At-a-glance also documents params (~220M) and that T5 is NOT a chat model.

conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual, VERIFIED: after the first-visit download, a same-profile reload auto-initialised to state=ready ('Model ready — running locally on your device.') with NO Download button (downloadButton=false) and 0 console errors. First visit correctly showed the Download control (download-required).
rubric.notes: Uses shared createModelLoader; no hand-rolled Load button.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, runs-without-webgpu-fallback all pass + honest-progress-retry-offline=manual. Download progress renders in the shared loader progress bar; run failures surface as an .err status; the createModelLoader Retry path covers recovery. WASM model needs no WebGPU.

conformance: controls-operable=manual. Verified controls: the task picker is a group of aria-pressed segmented buttons (translate DE/FR/RO, summarize, CoLA, STS-B) that swap the dynamic input fields; max-new-tokens + beams range sliders with live value labels; Run button; free-text/textarea fields per task. The 'exact string fed' box updates live as you type, highlighting the prefix scaffold. Driving the Run button produced the real translation above.
rubric.notes: STS-B swaps to two input fields; all controls labelled with visible <label> + focus-visible outlines.

rubric.evidence: conformance: no-fake-output=manual. The only output shown is streamed from the pipeline's generate(); empty output renders '(empty output)'. The 'exact fed string' + token chips are computed from the same buildInput() the worker uses, so what you read is what runs. No canned strings anywhere.

conformance: images-have-alt=pass (no ; icon is an inline SVG data URI), buttons-have-names=pass. Task picker uses role=group + aria-pressed; status regions use role=status aria-live; the decode-timeline canvas has an aria-label; every field has an associated
rubric.notes: Applied accessibility guidance: aria-pressed on the segmented task control, accessible names on all buttons.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) clean, mobile (360×740 DPR3) clean — no horizontal overflow on either class. Agent read screenshots (w17shots + reports/responsive/screens/t5-base-text2text/): light (warm paper) and dark both legible; header/lede/glance table wrap cleanly on 360px; the fed-input code box and token chips use word-break + min-inline-size:0 so long prefixed strings never overflow. Primary controls (task/run buttons) use min-block-size≈2.2rem.
rubric.notes: responsive-check flags 7–11 'sub-24px' targets — these are inline crumb/reference text links (standard, not primary tap targets); interactive control buttons are ≥~35px.

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs in a Web Worker (T5Engine → worker.js) and tokens stream via TextStreamer, so the control UI stays responsive during generation. The live 'fed string' + token rendering is O(tokens) and cheap.
rubric.notes: Applied identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks: off-main-thread inference + streamed incremental output.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap for 1D control rows; set min-inline-size:0 (and word-break) on flex/grid items holding long unbreakable content; use repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) for responsive control/stat grids.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the task picker and readout are flex-wrap rows; the fed-input string box and SentencePiece token chips use word-break:break-all + min-inline-size:0 so a long prefixed input never forces horizontal overflow on mobile; controls-grid and stat-row use auto-fit minmax.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose accessible names on all controls; use aria-pressed for toggle/segmented buttons; announce async state in a role=status aria-live region.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the task-prefix picker is a role=group of aria-pressed buttons; run/loader status is announced via role=status aria-live=polite; the timeline canvas has an aria-label.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names=pass; task-btn[aria-pressed] toggling verified in the headless run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy JS off the main thread and keep interaction handlers short to protect INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model download + generate run in a Web Worker; decoded tokens stream incrementally to the output; per-keystroke work (fed-string highlight) is O(input).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js drives pipeline().generate with a TextStreamer

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up / bound long main-thread loops so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation is off-thread and streamed; there is no long synchronous main-thread loop; token-chip render is bounded by the (short) input length.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: t5.js renderTokens over input tokens only; streaming callbacks update the DOM in small increments

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from public/styles.css @view-transition navigation:auto (invariant 6); use-case cards use the shared .model-card grid.

openQuestions: Is greedy the most honest default, or would beam search meaningfully improve translate/summarize quality at this size without hiding the mechanism?
Should the page add MNLI/other GLUE prefixes as additional tasks, or does the current four-task set best teach the prefix idea without clutter?
Would a side-by-side 'same input, T5 vs FLAN-T5' comparison be a valuable NEW additive demo to make the prefix-vs-instruction distinction concrete?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for t5-base-text2text is done (desktop real-output run + mobile/desktop light+dark screenshots read); support flipped to ok. Re-audit if the design system changes.
Add a NEW additive demo comparing task-prefix T5 against instruction-tuned FLAN-T5 on the same input, to make the 'prefix vs instruction' distinction concrete (new slug, never replacing this page).

summary: Revision-1 build critique for t5-base-text2text (text2text-generation, WASM q8, Xenova/t5-base), evidenced by a real headless full-lifecycle run: first-visit download → REAL output 'Das Haus ist wunderbar und der Garten ist ruhig.' for translate EN→DE; cached reload auto-inits with no Download button and 0 console errors; all four task prefixes verified (translate/summarize/CoLA/STS-B). The star is the multi-task-via-prefix mechanism, shown with the exact fed string highlighted. Conformance 14/14 auto-checkable pass + 9 manual-evidenced; responsive desktop+mobile clean, light+dark legible. Distinct from the instruction-tuned flan-t5-instruct demo.
